title: Hut 8 (HUT) Q1 Loss Of US$219.8 Million Tests Bullish Growth Narratives
text: Hut 8 Q1 2026 Earnings Analysis. Financial performance: Revenue $71.0 million with net loss of $219.8 million. Trailing 12-month results: $284.3 million in revenue against $311.4 million in losses. Profitability concerns: Quarterly net loss excluding extra items widened from $133.9 million in Q1 2025 to $219.8 million in Q1 2026 despite revenue more than tripling. Analysts expect Hut 8 to remain unprofitable through next three years, suggesting current losses may represent ongoing structural issue. Bullish growth narrative: Optimistic case rests on renewable energy contracts and AI/data center expansion justifying premium valuation. Currently trading at 43.1x price-to-sales versus software industry average of 3.7x. Bull narrative faces credibility questions: higher contracted coverage has not translated into positive earnings. Gap between growth expectations and persistent losses makes this investment fundamentally dependent on future margin improvement materializing. Revenue growth forecast: approximately 32% annually must overcome reality of current persistent unprofitability.
